Question: Getting MainEngineThread is returning 1603 Error in MSI Logs during installation of PowerPDF
Answer:
The Client moved the installation files (Deployment Script, *.MST) to a location other than the default and forgot to copy DATA1.CAB to the new location. DATA1.CAB is the PowerPDF installation program.
Copied the DATA1.CAB file to a new location, and PowerPDF installed properly.

Question: SharePoint connector doesn't see all folders in Power PDF
Answer:
In the contents, select the Settings of the library/folder they would like to check/set. Set the Content Types section, containing "Document" Content type in its list (there can be a list but the Document must be among them). If not, select "Advanced settings" and, in the Advanced Settings window, set the "Allow management content types" radio button to "Yes". After saving the setting the Content Types will appear in the Settings window, and the Folder/Library will appear in Power PDF SharePoint connector.
Power PDF version should be on minimum versions of 20205.3 and 5.1.1.14

Question: Who to contact for technical support on OEM versions of Kofax products.
Answer: If your license for Power PDF, Omnipage, or PaperPort was included with the purchase of a product from a 3rd party, such as a Scanner or Printer, you must contact that manufacturer for technical support. Due to contractual requirements Kofax is unable to provide direct technical support on OEM versions of our products.

Question: Power PDF Availability on Google Play Store
Answer:
We are aware that Power PDF is currently not available on the Google Play Store. Our team is actively investigating this issue and working towards a resolution.
An update will be provided in this Knowledge Base article once more information is available.
We appreciate your patience and understanding while this is being resolved.

Question: How to collect x32 and x64 bit log files for Power PDF 2025.3
Answer:
Purpose
One of the most effective ways to troubleshoot Power PDF issues is by collecting logs for our support team to review.
Before beginning, ensure that the File Name extension option is selected under the View option in Folder Options in Windows Explorer.
Open Folder
à
View
à
Check mark view File name extensions
Objective:
When Power PDF logging is needed, the log files for both x32 and x64 bit processing should be enabled
Step 1
Browse to the default directory where Power PDF software is installed on your system.
Typically, the file location would be:
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025
Step 2
2.1 If you see a file by name recdiag_log.ini with size 1 KB you may rename the file to recdiag_log_old.ini
2.2 Search and select RECDIAG_LOG.IN_ of size 13 KB at location C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025
then rename the file to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini'.
Note
– In some cases, renaming this file will require admin rights, but this depends on the customers environment and user permissions
32-Bit log steps
To also enable x86-bit logging mode, continue with the steps below and first b
rowse to the directory where Power PDF is installed on your system.
Typically, the file location would be C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025\x86
Step 3
If you see a file by name recdiag_log.ini with a size of 1 KB you may rename the file to recdiag_log_old.ini
Step 4
Search and select RECDIAG_LOG.IN_ of size 13 KB at location
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025\x86
then rename the file to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini'.
Step 5
5.1 Once the 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file is in place in both locations, create a folder named 'RECDIAG' in the C:\TEMP folder for the logs to be written out to
5.2 If this folder is not manually created before testing, the logs will not be created (C:\temp\recdiag).
5.3 Power PDF should start writing logs to C:\temp\recdiag as soon as the software is launched.
Step 6 -
Reproduce the issue and note the document name, date and time of the issue.
Collect the logs from C:\temp\recdiag after reproducing the issue successfully.
Share the logs with our support team.
Step 7
When testing is complete, be sure to rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' back to 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' so Power PDF does not continue logging,
